content.content:
1	# AGENTS.md — three-mmd-loader
2	
3	このファイルは AI コーディングエージェント (Codex / Claude Code / Cursor 等) 向けの
4	プロジェクト規約集です。**gitignore されています** — リポジトリには含まれません。
5	リポジトリを使う人間向け規約は README.md / ROADMAP.md を参照。
6	
7	人間が新しい規約を入れたい時は、このファイルを編集して各 AI ツールの brief で
8	最初に読ませてください。
9	
10	---
11	
12	## ハードルール (違反 = バグ扱い)
13	
14	### Hot path での allocation 禁止
15	`runtime.tick()` / `runtime.evaluate()` / `solver.solve*()` / `applyAppendTransforms` /
16	`applyMmdAnimation` などの **per-frame 経路** で:
17	
18	- ❌ `new THREE.Vector3()`, `new THREE.Quaternion()`, `new THREE.Matrix4()`
19	- ❌ `new Float32Array()`, `new Array()`, `[]` の new 生成 (push 累積)
20	- ❌ `.map()`, `.filter()`, `.slice()` で配列作成
21	- ✅ `DefaultMmdRuntime` 上の `scratch*` フィールドを `.copy()` / `.set()` で再利用
22	- ✅ 事前確保した typed array に index 書き込み
23	
24	### Geometry / texture
25	- ❌ `geometry.clone()` をフレームごと、または material 数分実行
26	- ✅ proxy mesh は attribute 参照共有 (`new BufferGeometry()` に attributes/index を参照代入)
27	- ✅ default texture wrap は `THREE.RepeatWrapping` (MMD UV 規約)
28	- ✅ Toon gradient は `ClampToEdgeWrapping` 維持
29	- ✅ default 経路で texture α scan を呼ばない (PMX flag で opaque 確定する場合)
30	
31	### Transparency 判定
32	- ✅ PMX flag (diffuse[3] < 1, alphaTest flag, evaluated transparency) を主軸
33	- ✅ texture α scan は `geometryAwareAlpha: true` のみ
34	- ❌ texture atlas の余白に α<255 があるだけで transparent: true にしない
35	
36	### API surface
37	- ✅ 公開 API (`src/three/index.ts`, `src/runtime/index.ts`, `src/parser/index.ts`,
38	       `src/physics/index.ts`) からの export だけが利用者向け
39	- ❌ internal helper を export しない (使われたら破壊的変更が困難になる)
40	- ✅ 破壊変更は deprecated alias を 1 リリース挟む
41	
42	---
43	
44	## 設計指針 (柔軟ルール、状況で判断)
45	
46	### DRY の濃度
47	AI コーディング時代は **重複の許容度が変わる**:
48	
49	| 対象 | 推奨 |
50	|---|---|
51	| 1-5 行の trivial helper (`lerp`, `clamp`, `cubicBezier`) | **co-locate OK** (使うファイルに直接書く) |
52	| 複雑な数学処理 (quaternion 補間、行列変換) | **共有モジュールに集約** (`runtime/math.ts` 等) |
53	| public API surface | **絶対に重複させない** (利用者が混乱) |
54	| Test 用 helper | **テストファイル内 co-locate**、必要なら test/helpers/ |
55	
56	理由: AI は abstraction の解決コストが人間より低いが、共有モジュールへの import +
57	シグネチャ確認に token を使う。trivial 1 行は inline の方が brief 効率高い。
58	
59	### ファイルサイズ目安
60	- `src/**`: モジュール 1 個あたり ~400 行が目安、500 超えたら分割検討
61	- `examples/viewer/lib/**`: 200-400 行
62	- `test/**`: 機能ごとに分割 (`SolverLimits.test.ts`, `SolverPrep.test.ts` 等)
63	- 分割は **type ではなく feature** で (× `types.ts` のみ巨大、○ `animation.ts` に
64	  関連型と関数を集約)
65	
66	### コメント
67	- ❌ コードを読めば自明な what (`// increment counter` 等)
68	- ✅ why-comments (制約、過去の incident、非自明な不変条件)
69	- ✅ workaround の context (issue #XX、specific browser bug 等)
70	- ❌ 「fix for #123」のような瞬間的 context (commit message に書く)
71	
72	### README 更新
73	- README の内容調整は **docs/README.ja.md を先に固める**。README.md から
74	  README.ja.md へ翻訳するのではなく、日本語版を ground truth として英語版
75	  README.md に反映する。
76	- ユーザーが README 文言を手動調整した場合、その意図を優先し、英語版へ反映する。
77	  消された補足や例を勝手に復活させない。
78	
79	### Release / Viewer deploy
80	- Release 作業では npm publish / GitHub Release で終わらせず、Viewer のデプロイも行う。
81	- 詳細なローカル手順と既知の blocker は `docs/DEPLOY.md` を参照する。
82	- `docs/DEPLOY.md` は `.git/info/exclude` で ignore される自分用 ground truth。
83	- `docs/RELEASE.md` にはこのローカル運用手順を書かない。
84	
85	---
86	
87	## Codex 委譲時の brief テンプレ追加要素
88	
89	`codex exec` を呼ぶ brief には次を含める:
90	
91	```text
92	# 必須参照
93	- AGENTS.md (最初に読む)
94	- README.md / ROADMAP.md
95	- 直近関連 commit (HEAD~5 程度の subject)
96	
97	# Hot path / API / Geometry を触る場合
98	- AGENTS.md の該当ハードルールを brief に **引用** する
99	- 変更後に該当ルール違反していないか SELF-REVIEW で明示
100	```
101	
102	---
103	
104	## Test 戦略
105	
106	### 単位
107	- **unit**: 1 関数 / 1 クラス / 純粋ロジック (`test/unit/**`)
108	- **integration**: 複数モジュール連携 (`test/integration/**`)
109	- **viewer source-level**: `test/unit/viewer/ViewerSource.test.ts` で文字列マッチ
110	  により viewer 構造を pin する (回帰防止)
111	
112	### 何を test するか
113	- ✅ 公開 API の behavior
114	- ✅ ハードルール違反の検出 (scratch 未使用、clone 出現 等)
115	- ❌ internal 構造のスナップショット (リファクタごとに壊れる)
116	- ❌ 実装詳細 (private method の signature 等)
117	
118	### Vitest が EPERM で落ちる
119	Windows + Codex sandbox 環境で Vitest 子プロセス spawn が `EPERM` で失敗することがある。
120	これは Codex sandbox 制約で本番では起きない。Codex の VERIFICATION では
121	`npm run build` + 直接実行可能なら手動 dist 確認で代替。**Claude Code 側で
122	最終 `npm test` を必ず通す**。
123	
124	---
125	
126	## Commit / PR
127	
128	### commit subject 規約
129	- `feat(scope): ...` 機能追加
130	- `fix(scope): ...` バグ修正
131	- `perf(scope): ...` 性能改善
132	- `refactor(scope): ...` 振る舞い変更なしの整理
133	- `test(scope): ...` テスト追加・修正
134	- `docs(scope): ...` 文書のみ
135	- `chore(scope): ...` build / CI / 設定
136	
137	scope は `runtime` / `three` / `parser` / `physics` / `viewer` / `readme` 等。
138	
139	### atomic commit
140	- 1 commit = 1 関心事
141	- AI 委譲時に "5 commit に分けて" と指示しても、shared file (index.ts 等) で
142	  分割困難な場合は **1 big commit** にまとめる方が安全 (subject に複数 scope を併記)
143	
144	### Co-Authored-By
145	AI が書いたコードを commit する場合、commit message 末尾に:
146	```
147	Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
148	```
149	または
150	```
151	Co-Authored-By: Codex GPT-5.5 <noreply@openai.com>
152	```
153	を追記する。
154	
155	---
156	
157	## このプロジェクトの最近の判断 (context)
158	
159	新しいセッションで AI が経緯を知っておくと役立つもの:
160	
161	- **AnimationClip 経路は完全削除済み** (commit e6d7dfc) — `MmdAnimation` のみ
162	- **string source は fetch でロード可** (commit c03891a) — `loadModel("/url.pmx")` OK
163	- **Bezier 補完実装済み** (commit 62fb52b)
164	- **append transform は PMX layer 順** (commit 2548f66)
165	- **IK chain validation は setAnimation で 1 回** (commit 820bc08) — solveChain 毎回はしない
166	- **outline / renderOrder proxy mesh は eager 生成** (commit 6457dca) — `added` event 廃止
167	- **renderOrderProxies は default OFF** (commit ae64922) — groups sort のみで足りる
168	- **transparency は PMX flag 優先** (commit a6f604b)
169	- **texture α scan は default skip** (commit f9ced5e)
170	- **disposeMmdModel / folder utility / loadAmmoNamespace を library export** (commit 0a6fde2)
171	
172	### 既知の制約
173	- Ammo.js 0.0.10 (asm.js) の 64MiB heap 固定
174	- Codex sandbox で `.git/index.lock` permission denied — commit は Claude Code 側で実行
175	- Codex の workspace-write BG を **並列実行しない** (workspace 競合)
176	- Codex output には [BLOCKED: Base64...] が混じる (MCP の安全機能)
177	
178	---
179	
180	## このファイルの更新
181	
182	新規規約・判断・教訓は **このファイルに追加** していくこと。
183	AI セッションで「同じ ground truth を共有する」ためのファイル。
184	
185	人間が削除したいルールは削除、追加したい時は append。
186	gitignore されているため git では追跡されない — チーム共有が必要なら
187	README.md / ROADMAP.md / CHANGELOG.md に移す。
188

content.content:
1	# Roadmap
2	
3	Local-only planning notes for upcoming `@yohawing/three-mmd-loader` work.
4	
5	Keep this file shaped like `CHANGELOG.md`: group work by target version, then
6	by intent. Move completed items into `CHANGELOG.md` when they ship.
7	
8	## Unreleased
9	
10	### Triage
11	
12	- Decide whether each item below should stay as a local roadmap item, become a
13	  GitHub issue, or be deferred until a breaking-release planning pass.
14	- Keep public README and `docs/README.ja.md` focused on the current supported
15	  surface; do not restore public roadmap links unless the package needs a
16	  public planning document again.
17	
18	## [0.4.0] - Planned
19	
20	### Changed
21	
22	- Remove Ammo.js from the default guidance and examples. Keep the backend
23	  available only as a compatibility path unless a breaking-release decision
24	  removes it entirely.
25	- Promote the MMD-optimized Bullet backend as the only recommended physics
26	  path in README, `docs/README.ja.md`, and viewer-facing examples.
27	- Resolve the remaining mmd-anim adapter parity gaps so PMX loading no longer
28	  needs the load-time TypeScript parser fallback in `MmdAnimBackedCore`.
29	- Bring the TypeScript runtime behavior closer to the mmd-anim/WASM runtime,
30	  especially for animation sampling, append transforms, IK, morph sync, and
31	  physics handoff semantics.
32	- Improve TypeScript runtime performance while preserving the hot-path
33	  no-allocation rules for `tick`, `evaluate`, IK solving, append transform
34	  application, and animation application.
35	- Improve model loading performance across source fetch, PMX/PMD parse,
36	  geometry/material assembly, texture resolution, and runtime initialization.
37	- Add an asset dependency resolver that can collect and resolve textures,
38	  motions, camera motions, audio, accessories, and project-relative paths across
39	  PMX/PMD/PMM/accessory workflows.
40	- Add initial accessory support, starting with an explicit scope decision for
41	  `.x` accessories, `.vac` placement metadata, viewer loading UX, and whether
42	  accessory attachment belongs in the loader, runtime, or an example/helper
43	  layer.
44	- Add partial PMM project support beyond metadata parsing, starting with
45	  extracting project asset references and enough placement/playback state to
46	  drive viewer loading for models, motions, camera, audio, and accessories.
47	- Tune the current MMD-compatible shading path by comparing rendered output
48	  against MMD dump/reference images, focusing on toon response, diffuse/specular
49	  balance, ambient terms, alpha handling, outline appearance, and shadow
50	  interaction.
51	- Strengthen camera, light, and self-shadow runtime parity by comparing VMD
52	  sampled state and rendered output against MMD dump/reference evidence.
53	- Add focused API recipes for common integration paths: model + VMD + camera +
54	  physics, local files/folders, URL loading, custom runtime, custom physics, and
55	  viewer/local asset workflows.
56	- Add a kuroko model preset to the viewer/local asset workflow after confirming
57	  redistribution terms, attribution text, and whether the model can be bundled
58	  or must remain a local-only fixture reference.
59	- Improve diagnostics so loader/runtime/viewer code can distinguish missing
60	  textures, unsupported texture formats, WASM fallback, unsupported material
61	  features, skipped physics, accessory resolution failures, and PMM partial
62	  support gaps with structured data.
63	- Evolve the viewer into a verification tool with fixed-frame seek,
64	  diagnostics panels, before/after image capture, MMD dump overlay/reference
65	  comparison, and repeatable local asset presets.
66	
67	### Fixed
68	
69	- Investigate the local fixture oracle mismatches currently skipped in
70	  `test/fixtures/fixtures.local.json`, especially the Phase5 rig regression
71	  candidates around PMX knee link-limit/local-axis behavior and dense REM
72	  IK/twist rigs.
73	- Separate true runtime IK regressions from native-oracle limitations where
74	  the local oracle does not apply VMD property IK enable states.
75	
76	### Test
77	
78	- Audit the current test suite and classify cases by purpose: public API
79	  behavior, parser/runtime parity, visual regression, release gate, local-only
80	  asset verification, and implementation-rule checks.
81	- Remove or rewrite tests that only pin fragile internal structure without
82	  protecting meaningful public behavior.
83	- Add missing coverage for supported behavior that is currently only protected
84	  by manual viewer checks or local fixture runs.
85	- Keep local-only fixture tests clearly separated from portable CI/release
86	  tests so release confidence does not depend on user-owned assets.
87	- Redefine the release gate after the test audit, explicitly separating
88	  required portable checks from optional local visual/playback evidence:
89	  build, lint, unit/integration tests, `smoke:dist`, `smoke:types`, fixture
90	  checks, visual smoke, and local-only smoke.
91	
92	### Verification
93	
94	- Re-run local playback stability coverage after each rig fix and only remove
95	  `skipReason` entries when the native-oracle expectation is known to be
96	  meaningful for that case.
97	- Keep Sour PMX plus `ラビットホール.vmd` as a high-signal regression fixture for
98	  IK limit and knee-instability checks.
99	- Compare TypeScript runtime output against the mmd-anim/WASM runtime on
100	  generated fixtures and selected local playback fixtures before claiming
101	  parity improvements.
102	- Track performance changes with runtime benchmarks and allocation-oriented
103	  source review before accepting hot-path changes.
104	- Track model loading performance with repeatable benchmarks and viewer timing
105	  diagnostics before and after parser, texture, or assembly changes.
106	- Verify asset dependency resolution against portable fixtures and local project
107	  folders before using it as the foundation for PMM/accessory workflows.
108	- Validate accessory support with portable minimal fixtures first, then local
109	  real accessory/model combinations in the viewer before documenting the API.
110	- Validate partial PMM support with minimal portable PMM fixtures and local
111	  project files, documenting unsupported fields instead of implying full MMD
112	  project compatibility.
113	- Capture before/after image evidence when changing shading behavior and keep
114	  visual tolerances narrow enough to catch regressions without overfitting to a
115	  single local model.
116	
117	## [0.5.0] - Planned
118	
119	### Design
120	
121	- Decide whether a rendering pipeline belongs in this package or should live in
122	  a companion package/example layer. The decision should account for API
123	  stability, dependency weight, renderer-specific code, and whether consumers
124	  expect this project to remain a loader/runtime library.
125	- Sketch a pipeline boundary for MMD image making before adding shading helpers:
126	  scene lighting, material conversion/enhancement, outline/self-shadow,
127	  post-processing, renderer selection, and viewer presets should have explicit
128	  ownership.
129	- Consider an opt-in `MmdRenderingPipeline` style API only if it can compose
130	  with existing `ThreeMmdLoader` output without taking over the user's whole
131	  Three.js scene.
132	
133	### Added
134	
135	- Explore a Three.js WebGPU rendering path for MMD models, keeping the existing
136	  WebGL path as the default until material, outline, morph, skinning, and
137	  self-shadow behavior have parity evidence.
138	- Prototype WGSL-backed shader pieces where they provide a clear benefit over
139	  Three.js' built-in WebGPU node/material path, especially for MMD-specific
140	  material evaluation, toon handling, or future skinning/runtime experiments.
141	- Evaluate style-helper APIs for richer MMD shading presets inspired by rayMMD
142	  and sd PBR workflows, without claiming drop-in compatibility with those
143	  effect stacks.
144	- Prototype opt-in material/lighting helpers for PBR-like image making:
145	  environment lighting setup, shadow tuning, toon-to-PBR material parameter
146	  mapping, rim/specular controls, and post-processing-friendly defaults.
147	
148	### Verification
149	
150	- Validate the pipeline ownership decision with at least one app-style viewer
151	  prototype before committing to public API.
152	- Add visual parity checks that compare WebGL and WebGPU output for generated
153	  PMX material cases before exposing WebGPU as a documented viewer option.
154	- Extend the viewer verification workflow to switch WebGL/WebGPU paths and
155	  capture comparable frames for renderer and shading experiments.
156	- Treat WebGPU support as experimental until browser support, Three.js API
157	  stability, and package ergonomics are confirmed against typed consumer smoke
158	  coverage.
159	- Validate shading helpers with real MMD models in the local viewer and keep
160	  them opt-in so the default MMD-compatible material path remains stable.
161	
162	## [1.0.0] - Planned
163	
164	### Removed
165	
166	- Remove deprecated aliases after one release window:
167	  `ThreeMmdModel.object`, `ThreeMmdModel.textureDiagnostics`,
168	  `ThreeMmdLoadModelOptions.outlines`, and
169	  `ThreeMmdLoadModelOptions.renderOrderProxies`.
170	- Remove or replace deprecated runtime APIs only after confirming downstream
171	  consumers have a clear migration path:
172	  `tick(seconds, mesh, options)` and broad `stop()` usage.
173	- Decide whether `AmmoPhysicsBackendTuningOptions.additionalDampingPatch`
174	  should be removed with the rest of the deprecated compatibility aliases.
175	
176	### Changed
177	
178	- Revisit the public physics API once the default path is firmly the direct
179	  MMD Bullet backend and the legacy Ammo.js path is no longer part of standard
180	  documentation.
181	
182	### Verification
183	
184	- Before cutting the breaking release, run the release gate with typed consumer
185	  smoke coverage across root and subpath imports.
186

content.content:
1	# Release
2	
3	This checklist is the operator-facing release procedure for
4	`@yohawing/three-mmd-loader`. Development details for individual scripts live in
5	[DEVELOPMENT.md](./DEVELOPMENT.md).
6	
7	## 1. Preflight
8	
9	- Confirm the target version and release scope.
10	- Confirm `CHANGELOG.md` has an entry for the target version.
11	- Build the `CHANGELOG.md` entry from the commits since the previous release
12	  tag, not only from the final release-prep commit.
13	- Confirm `package.json` `version` matches the intended release.
14	- Confirm the working tree is clean.
15	- Confirm the release branch is based on `develop`.
16	- Confirm npm Trusted Publishing is configured for the GitHub `npm`
17	  environment.
18	
19	```bash
20	git status --short
21	npm ci
22	```
23	
24	## 2. Local Checks
25	
26	Run the same release-blocking checks that CI runs before packaging:
27	
28	```bash
29	npm run lint
30	npm test
31	npm run build
32	npm run check:fixtures
33	npm run smoke:dist
34	npm run smoke:types
35	npm pack --dry-run --json
36	```
37	
38	If the `native/third_party/mmd-anim` submodule or the WASM export surface changed,
39	rebuild the WASM wrapper before the normal build:
40	
41	```bash
42	npm run build:mmd-anim
43	npm run build
44	```
45	
46	If this release changes rendering (material/outline/shader) or the mmd-anim
47	parser/runtime/WASM artifacts, also refresh and review the generated-PMX visual
48	baselines before tagging. They are **not** CI-gated (GPU/platform-specific
49	output, see [DEVELOPMENT.md](./DEVELOPMENT.md) → Visual Regression Scripts) and
50	can otherwise ship stale:
51	
52	```bash
53	npm run visual:smoke:generated-pmx
54	# if rendering legitimately changed: regenerate, review the PNG diffs, re-run
55	# the smoke until green, then commit the reviewed baselines
56	npm run render:visual:generated-pmx:baseline
57	```
58	
59	## 3. Commit
60	
61	Commit the version, changelog, and any release-prep changes before tagging.
62	
63	```bash
64	git status --short
65	git add package.json package-lock.json CHANGELOG.md
66	git commit -m "chore(release): vX.Y.Z"
67	```
68	
69	Adjust the staged files if the release includes additional documentation or
70	source changes.
71	
72	Push the release-prep commit to `develop` before opening the release PR.
73	
74	```bash
75	git push origin develop
76	```
77	
78	## 4. Merge `develop` to `main`
79	
80	Open a pull request from `develop` to `main` before tagging. Do not publish from
81	`develop`; the release tag must be created from the reviewed and merged `main`
82	commit.
83	
84	```bash
85	gh pr create --base main --head develop --title "chore(release): vX.Y.Z"
86	```
87	
88	Review the PR, wait for CI, and merge it into `main`. Direct pushes to `main`
89	may be blocked by repository rules; use the PR path even when the merge is a
90	fast-forward.
91	
92	After merging, update the local `main` branch and confirm that `package.json`
93	still matches the intended version.
94	
95	```bash
96	git fetch origin
97	git switch main
98	git pull --ff-only origin main
99	node -e "const p=require('./package.json'); if (p.version !== 'X.Y.Z') process.exit(1)"
100	```
101	
102	## 5. Tag
103	
104	The release workflow requires the Git tag to match `package.json` exactly.
105	Create the tag on `main` after the release PR has been reviewed and merged.
106	
107	```bash
108	git tag vX.Y.Z
109	git push origin vX.Y.Z
110	```
111	
112	For example, `package.json` version `0.2.0` must be tagged as `v0.2.0`.
113	
114	## 6. GitHub Actions
115	
116	The `Release` workflow runs on `v*.*.*` tags. It builds the package, validates
117	tag/version consistency, creates the npm tarball, publishes to npm, and creates
118	or updates the GitHub Release.
119	
120	Verify these workflow results:
121	
122	- The package job passed `lint`, `test`, `build`, fixture checks, dist smoke,
123	  and TypeScript consumer smoke.
124	- The publish job ran under the GitHub `npm` environment.
125	- npm Trusted Publishing succeeded.
126	- The GitHub Release exists for the tag, has the package artifact attached, and
127	  uses the matching `CHANGELOG.md` version section as its release notes.
128	
129	The release workflow extracts the `## [X.Y.Z]` section from `CHANGELOG.md` for
130	tag `vX.Y.Z`. If that section is missing, the workflow fails instead of
131	publishing generic generated notes.
132	
133	Manual dispatch is also available, but use tag-triggered releases for normal
134	publishing so the GitHub Release is tied to the version tag.
135	
136	## 7. Post-release Verification
137	
138	Verify the published npm artifact rather than only the local package:
139	
140	```bash
141	npm view @yohawing/three-mmd-loader version
142	npm view @yohawing/three-mmd-loader peerDependencies
143	npm view @yohawing/three-mmd-loader dist-tags
144	```
145	
146	When compatibility matters for a downstream app, install the published version
147	there or in a temporary consumer before updating that app.
148

1026	function generatePmx({
1027	  name,
1028	  englishName,
1029	  comment,
1030	  englishComment,
1031	  bones,
1032	  morphs,
1033	  geometry,
1034	  materials,
1035	  textures,
1036	  textEncoding = "utf8",
1037	  indexSizes = defaultIndexSizes()
1038	}) {
1039	  const writer = new BinaryWriter(textEncoding);
1040	
1041	  writer.bytes(new TextEncoder().encode("PMX "));
1042	  writer.f32(2.0);
1043	  writer.u8(8);
1044	  writer.u8(textEncoding === "utf16le" ? 0 : 1);
1045	  writer.u8(0);
1046	  writer.u8(indexSizes.vertex);
1047	  writer.u8(indexSizes.texture);
1048	  writer.u8(indexSizes.material);
1049	  writer.u8(indexSizes.bone);
1050	  writer.u8(indexSizes.morph);
1051	  writer.u8(indexSizes.rigidBody);
1052	
1053	  writer.text(name);
1054	  writer.text(englishName);
1055	  writer.text(comment);
1056	  writer.text(englishComment);
1057	
1058	  writeVertices(writer, geometry.vertices, indexSizes);
1059	  writeFaces(writer, geometry.indices, indexSizes);
1060	  writeTextures(writer, textures);
1061	  writeMaterials(writer, materials, indexSizes);
1062	  writeBones(writer, bones, indexSizes);
1063	  writeMorphs(writer, morphs, indexSizes);
1064	  writeDisplayFrames(writer, bones, morphs, indexSizes);
1065	  writer.i32(0);
1066	  writer.i32(0);
1067	
1068	  return writer.toUint8Array();
1069	}

1071	async function main() {
1072	  if (process.argv.includes("--list-rest-pose-cases")) {
1073	    console.log(restPoseCaseIds().join("\n"));
1074	    return;
1075	  }
1076	
1077	  if (process.argv.includes("--list-visual-cases")) {
1078	    console.log(visualCaseIds().join("\n"));
1079	    return;
1080	  }
1081	
1082	  if (process.argv.includes("--list-self-shadow-cases")) {
1083	    console.log(selfShadowCaseIds().join("\n"));
1084	    return;
1085	  }
1086	
1087	  if (process.argv.includes("--all-rest-pose")) {
1088	    const outputArgIndex = process.argv.indexOf("--output-dir");
1089	    const outputDir =
1090	      outputArgIndex >= 0 && process.argv[outputArgIndex + 1] !== undefined
1091	        ? process.argv[outputArgIndex + 1]
1092	        : REST_POSE_OUTPUT_DIR;
1093	    for (const caseId of restPoseCaseIds()) {
1094	      const outputPath = resolve(outputDir, `${caseId}.pmx`);
1095	      const bytes = generateRestPosePmx(caseId);
1096	      await mkdir(dirname(outputPath), { recursive: true });
1097	      await writeFile(outputPath, bytes);
1098	      console.log(`wrote ${bytes.byteLength} bytes to ${outputPath}`);
1099	    }
1100	    return;
1101	  }
1102	
1103	  if (process.argv.includes("--all-visual")) {

content.content:
1	# Generated PMX fixtures
2	
3	`minimal-loader-smoke.pmx` is a deterministic, redistribution-safe PMX generated by `scripts/fixtures/generate-minimal-pmx.mjs`; it represents a tiny texture-free tetrahedron-like skinned mesh with one material, three bones, and one vertex morph.
4	
5	Generated PMX files include MMD-compatible special display frames (`Root` and `表情`) so they can be opened by MikuMikuDance for dumper oracle generation.
6	
7	`rest-pose/*.pmx` fixtures are generated by the same script with `--all-rest-pose`; they cover append rotate, append local, IK chain, fixed/local axis, and transform-after-physics bone metadata for rest-pose identity regression tests.
8	
9	`visual/*.pmx` fixtures are generated by the same script with `--all-visual`; they cover small redistribution-safe PMX scenes for visual regression paths. `mmd-material-order-body-outline-interleave.pmx` contains two overlapping angled translucent edged thin boxes split across two PMX materials; `mmd-outline-normal-silhouette.pmx` contains one rotated translucent edged box for outline normal/depth visibility checks; `mmd-texture-alpha-used-uv-cutout.pmx` contains one textured opaque PMX material whose used UVs sample alpha cutout pixels; `mmd-texture-alpha-atlas-padding-ignored.pmx` contains one textured opaque PMX material whose used UVs stay inside an opaque atlas region while unused padding is transparent.
10

## Summary of Scripts and Fixtures

### File Structure Overview

**scripts/visual-regression/** (11 files):
- render-real-models.mjs
- render-local-self-shadow-pair.mjs
- render-shared.mjs
- compute-metrics.mjs
- compute-shadow-metrics.mjs
- compare-real-models-rest-pose.mjs
- snapshot-real-models-rest-pose.mjs
- real-models.manifest.json
- self-shadow.manifest.json
- generated-pmx.manifest.json
- skinning.manifest.json

**scripts/fixtures/** (2 files):
- generate-minimal-pmx.mjs
- generate-minimal-vmd.mjs

---

### File Details

**F:\Develop\MMDDev\three-mmd-loader\scripts\visual-regression\render-real-models.mjs**
- Main rendering script for visual regression testing with real MMD models
- Launches Chromium browser via Playwright, renders models with Three.js
- Supports shadow rendering with configureMmdSelfShadowDirectionalLight
- Outputs PNG images with SHA256 hashes for regression detection
- Uses static HTTP server to serve MMD assets from local data root

**F:\Develop\MMDDev\three-mmd-loader\scripts\visual-regression\render-local-self-shadow-pair.mjs**
- Specialized renderer for self-shadow testing: renders same model twice (shadow on/off)
- Generates side-by-side pair images and cropped difference visualizations
- Calculates darkening metrics (mean, p95, max) for quality validation
- Configurable camera, motion timing, and output directory via environment variables or CLI args

**F:\Develop\MMDDev\three-mmd-loader\scripts\visual-regression\render-shared.mjs**
- Utility module exporting browserLaunchOptions() and sha256File()
- Locates Chrome/Edge on Windows from standard installation paths
- Respects PLAYWRIGHT_CHROMIUM_EXECUTABLE_PATH environment variable

**F:\Develop\MMDDev\three-mmd-loader\scripts\visual-regression\compute-metrics.mjs**
- Compares baseline vs current PNG images using perceptual distance (JS metric or NVIDIA FLIP)
- Generates heatmap diff images and reports pass/fail by threshold
- Supports three visual regression profiles: real-models, generated-pmx, skinning
- Uses sRGB→linear color conversion for accurate perceptual metrics

**F:\Develop\MMDDev\three-mmd-loader\scripts\visual-regression\compute-shadow-metrics.mjs**
- Validates self-shadow visual quality by comparing shadow-on vs shadow-off renders
- Measures darkening in region-of-interest (ROI) with P95 percentile metric
- Checks shadow pixel ratio and prevents shadow collapse on black toons
- Reports detailed darkening/luminance values per comparison case

**F:\Develop\MMDDev\three-mmd-loader\scripts\visual-regression\compare-real-models-rest-pose.mjs**
- Compares bone rotations (quaternions) between baseline and current rest-pose snapshots
- Validates that bone animations haven't drifted beyond threshold (default 0.1°)
- Checks local and world quaternion angles separately
- Monitors watchBones array with defaults: センター, 腰, 下半身, 上半身

**F:\Develop\MMDDev\three-mmd-loader\scripts\visual-regression\snapshot-real-models-rest-pose.mjs**
- Creates JSON snapshots of bone quaternions at rest pose (t=0)
- Uses Three.js to load PMX, extract skeleton bone data
- Stores local and world quaternions for later diff comparison
- Generates snapshots for CI baseline establishment

**F:\Develop\MMDDev\three-mmd-loader\scripts\visual-regression\real-models.manifest.json**
- Placeholder manifest for local real-model visual regression
- Requires user-provided assets under MMD_DATA_ROOT
- Render settings: 512×512px, 1.2 ambient intensity, 1.8 directional intensity at [2.5, 5, 4]
- No models/motions committed; tests skip if assets missing

**F:\Develop\MMDDev\three-mmd-loader\scripts\visual-regression\self-shadow.manifest.json**
- Visual TDD manifest for generated self-shadow PMX cases (12 test cases)
- Defines shadow render parameters: 512×512, 2.25× directional intensity, advanced shadow map settings
- Comparison ROI checks: body torso, body with VMD, black-toon collapse, caster/receiver flags mixed states
- Thresholds: 0.0015 mean darkening min, 0.006 p95 darkening min (prevents no-op implementations)

**F:\Develop\MMDDev\three-mmd-loader\scripts\visual-regression\generated-pmx.manifest.json**
- Visual regression for 18 generated in-repo PMX test cases
- Tests diffuse lighting, toon ramps, alpha blending, sphere textures, edge rendering, alpha cutout
- Resolution 1024×1024, default threshold mean=0.03, p95=0.12
- Cases cover material morphs, texture atlasing, outline ordering, hair shadows

**F:\Develop\MMDDev\three-mmd-loader\scripts\visual-regression\skinning.manifest.json**
- 4 deformation test cases: BDEF2 bend 90°, SDEF bend 90°, BDEF4 twist 120°, QDEF twist 120°
- Generated by generate-minimal-pmx.mjs with --all-skinning flag
- Validates joint deformation accuracy (linear vs spherical vs dual-quaternion)

**F:\Develop\MMDDev\three-mmd-loader\scripts\fixtures\generate-minimal-pmx.mjs** (partial, 1688+ lines)
- Factory for generating minimal but comprehensive PMX test fixtures
- Generates 5 REST_POSE_CASES with appendRotate, IK chains, fixed axes, physics metadata
- Generates 6 SKINNING_CASES covering BDEF1/2/4, SDEF, QDEF skinning types
- Generates 18 VISUAL_CASES: lighting, materials, textures, transparency, outline ordering
- Generates 8 SELF_SHADOW_CASES with flags for caster/receiver control, probe geometry, black toons
- Custom toon texture generation (stepwise gradients, radial sphere maps, alpha overlays)

**F:\Develop\MMDDev\three-mmd-loader\scripts\fixtures\generate-minimal-vmd.mjs**
- Generates minimal VMD animation files
- Binary layout: signature, model name, bone frames with quaternions, self-shadow frames
- Exports generateMinimalVmd() factory with quaternion per bone, linear bezier interpolation
- Used for skinning and self-shadow animation test cases

---

### Key Integration Points

**Self-Shadow Rendering Pipeline:**
- render-real-models.mjs imports applyMmdSelfShadowStateToThreeDirectionalLight, configureMmdSelfShadowDirectionalLight, fitMmdSelfShadowDirectionalLightToBox from dist/three/index.js
- Samples selfShadowFrames from VMD animation via sampleMmdSelfShadowTrack
- Validates metrics in compute-shadow-metrics.mjs with per-case thresholds

**Camera & Lighting:**
- "front-fit" mode: OrthographicCamera fitted to bounding box with 1.18× margin
- "viewer-fit" mode: PerspectiveCamera at 22° FOV, positioned 5.2× model radius away
- Custom camera config: explicit position, target, FOV, near/far planes

**Test Fixtures:**
- All generated PMX/VMD use standard binary formats (PMX 2.0, VMD)
- Minimal geometry: single bone quads, cylinders, boxes with computed normals
- Material morphs, texture assets embedded (PNG/TGA with custom palettes)

summary: Explore unit and integration tests for camera/light/self-shadow VMD

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have comprehensive information. Let me compile the final report:

## Test Infrastructure Investigation Summary

### Overview
The three-mmd-loader repository has a well-structured test infrastructure (58 test files total) with dedicated testing for camera, light, and self-shadow VMD functionality. These features are critical for MMD animation playback in Three.js.

---

## 1. TEST FILES STRUCTURE

### Unit Tests: Test Core Functionality
**F:/Develop/MMDDev/three-mmd-loader/test/unit/**

**Camera & Light Sampling:**
- **F:/Develop/MMDDev/three-mmd-loader/test/unit/runtime/CameraLightSampling.test.ts** - Core sampling tests for camera frames, light frames, and self-shadow frames with interpolation validation
  - `sampleMmdCameraTrack()` - samples camera with channel-specific Bezier interpolation (distance, position XYZ, rotation, FOV)
  - `sampleMmdLightTrack()` - samples light linearly (color RGB, direction)
  - `sampleMmdSelfShadowTrack()` - samples self-shadow as held states (mode 0/1, distance)
  - Tests frame-index hints for forward playback optimization
  - Tests one-frame camera cuts behavior

**Camera Three.js Integration:**
- **F:/Develop/MMDDev/three-mmd-loader/test/unit/three/Camera.test.ts** - Camera state application to Three.js
  - `applyMmdCameraStateToThreeCamera()` - applies MMD camera state with coordinate conversion
  - Tests perspective vs orthographic camera switching
  - Tests camera roll (Z rotation) preservation
  - Tests camera orbit direction (Y rotation without mirroring)
  - Tests camera target aiming at MMD camera center
  - Tests outside parent world position addition
  - Tests screen-space projection goldens for inverse MMD rotations

**Self-Shadow Lighting:**
- **F:/Develop/MMDDev/three-mmd-loader/test/unit/three/Shadow.test.ts** - Self-shadow directional light configuration
  - `applyMmdSelfShadowStateToThreeDirectionalLight()` - enables/disables shadow casting by mode
  - `configureMmdSelfShadowDirectionalLight()` - configures quality (map size, bias, intensity, camera bounds)
  - `fitMmdSelfShadowDirectionalLightToBox()` - fits shadow camera to world-space bounding box
  - Tests shadow intensity clamping for toon blending
  - Tests light orientation preservation while fitting
  - Tests margin and depth margin application

**VMD Parser Tests:**
- **F:/Develop/MMDDev/three-mmd-loader/test/unit/parser/vmd/VmdParser.test.ts** - VMD parsing including camera/light sections
  - Tests camera frame interpolation in channel-strided byte layout (6 channels × 4 bezier points each)
  - Tests handling of optional sections (camera, light, self-shadow, property)
  - Tests morph-only VMD (ends before camera section)
  - Tests explicit camera count 0 followed by EOF

- **F:/Develop/MMDDev/three-mmd-loader/test/unit/parser/vmd/VmdMetadataParser.test.ts** - VMD metadata parsing
  - Tests section counts for bones, morphs, cameras, lights, self-shadows, properties
  - Tests self-shadow and property section absence detection
  - Tests truncated payload rejection
  - Tests trailing bytes preservation

**Runtime Tests:**
- **F:/Develop/MMDDev/three-mmd-loader/test/unit/runtime/DefaultMmdRuntime.test.ts**
  - Tests `cameraState()` and `lightState()` exposure from current animation
  - Tests render-sync skeleton updates with frame state

- **F:/Develop/MMDDev/three-mmd-loader/test/unit/runtime/MmdAnimRuntime.test.ts** - WASM-backed animation runtime

**Material & Shadow Tests:**
- **F:/Develop/MMDDev/three-mmd-loader/test/unit/three/Materials.test.ts** - Material flag handling including `selfShadow` and `selfShadowMap`
- **F:/Develop/MMDDev/three-mmd-loader/test/unit/three/Outline.test.ts** - Uses `MMD_SELF_SHADOW_LAYER` constant

---

## 2. VISUAL REGRESSION TEST INFRASTRUCTURE

### Self-Shadow Manifest
**F:/Develop/MMDDev/three-mmd-loader/scripts/visual-regression/self-shadow.manifest.json** - Comprehensive self-shadow test profile
- 12 test cases covering:
  - Body self-shadow on/off/caster-off variations
  - Black toon material self-shadow
  - VMD-controlled self-shadow (mode toggle via animation)
  - SDEF depth calculations
  - Receiver/caster flag combinations
- Comparison metrics:
  - `receiverMeanDarkeningMin` - shadow darkening detection
  - `receiverP95DarkeningMin` - 95th percentile darkening
  - `shadowPixelRatioMin` - shadow coverage
  - `shadowOnMeanLuminanceMin` - avoids collapse to black
  - `outsideRoiMeanDeltaMax` - prevents image-wide changes

### Rendering Scripts
- **F:/Develop/MMDDev/three-mmd-loader/scripts/visual-regression/render-local-self-shadow-pair.mjs** - Renders on/off pairs for diff comparison
- **F:/Develop/MMDDev/three-mmd-loader/scripts/visual-regression/compute-shadow-metrics.mjs** - Computes darkening/coverage metrics
- **F:/Develop/MMDDev/three-mmd-loader/scripts/visual-regression/render-real-models.mjs** - Renders using manifest profiles

---

## 3. TEST FIXTURES

### Generated Self-Shadow Fixtures
**F:/Develop/MMDDev/three-mmd-loader/test/fixtures/generated/self-shadow/**

**PMX Models (9 variants):**
- `mmd-self-shadow-on.pmx` - Full self-shadow enabled
- `mmd-self-shadow-body-on.pmx` - Body self-shadow only
- `mmd-self-shadow-body-caster-off.pmx` - Body with caster disabled
- `mmd-self-shadow-body-black-toon-on.pmx` - Black toon material variation
- `mmd-self-shadow-body-black-toon-caster-off.pmx` - Black toon with caster disabled
- `mmd-self-shadow-caster-flag-off-mixed.pmx` - Mixed caster flags
- `mmd-self-shadow-receiver-flag-off-mixed.pmx` - Mixed receiver flags
- `mmd-self-shadow-scale-floor-caster.pmx` - Scale/floor caster variation
- `mmd-self-shadow-sdef-depth.pmx` - SDEF depth test variant

**VMD Animations (3):**
- `mmd-self-shadow-vmd-on.vmd` - Enables self-shadow mode 1 at frame 0
- `mmd-self-shadow-vmd-off.vmd` - Disables self-shadow mode 0 at frame 0
- `mmd-self-shadow-sdef-depth.vmd` - Animation for SDEF depth testing

### Standard Fixtures
**F:/Develop/MMDDev/three-mmd-loader/test/fixtures/**

- `test_append_bone.vmd` - Append bone animation

---

## 4. IMPLEMENTATION ARCHITECTURE

### VMD Data Types
**F:/Develop/MMDDev/three-mmd-loader/src/parser/model/modelTypes.ts**
```
VmdCameraFrame: { frame, distance, position[3], rotation[3], fov, perspective, interpolation }
VmdLightFrame: { frame, color[3], direction[3] }
VmdSelfShadowFrame: { frame, mode (0/1), distance }
```

### Sampling API (Public)
**F:/Develop/MMDDev/three-mmd-loader/src/runtime/animation.ts**
- `sampleMmdCameraTrack(frames, frame): CameraState?` - Returns sampled camera state
- `sampleMmdCameraTrackInto(frames, frame, target, hint?): CameraState?` - Reusable target object
- `sampleMmdLightTrack(frames, frame): LightState?` - Linear light sampling
- `sampleMmdLightTrackInto(frames, frame, target): LightState?` - Reusable target
- `sampleMmdSelfShadowTrack(frames, frame): SelfShadowState?` - Held state (no interpolation)
- `sampleMmdSelfShadowTrackInto(frames, frame, target, hint?): SelfShadowState?` - Reusable target

### Runtime Integration
**F:/Develop/MMDDev/three-mmd-loader/src/runtime/core.ts (DefaultMmdRuntime)**
- `cameraState(): CameraState?` - Exposes current camera frame
- `lightState(): LightState?` - Exposes current light frame
- Frame-index hints cached in `scratchCameraFrameHint` for forward playback

### Three.js Application
**F:/Develop/MMDDev/three-mmd-loader/src/three/camera.ts**
- `applyMmdCameraStateToThreeCamera(camera, state, options?)` - Converts MMD camera to Three.js
- Handles coordinate system conversion (MMD: Y-up, Z-back vs Three.js: Y-up, Z-forward)
- Supports perspective + orthographic camera switching
- Applies world position offsets for parent bone attachment

**F:/Develop/MMDDev/three-mmd-loader/src/three/shadow.ts**
- `applyMmdSelfShadowStateToThreeDirectionalLight(light, state, options?)` - Applies shadow state
- `configureMmdSelfShadowDirectionalLight(light, options?)` - Batch quality configuration
- `fitMmdSelfShadowDirectionalLightToBox(light, box, options?)` - Auto-fit shadow camera
- `MMD_SELF_SHADOW_LAYER = 1` - Reserved layer for shadow geometry

---

## 5. KEY TEST PATTERNS & COVERAGE

### Interpolation Testing
- **Bezier interpolation** for camera (6 channels: distance, positionX/Y/Z, rotation, FOV)
- **Linear interpolation** for light (color RGB, direction)
- **Held state** for self-shadow (no interpolation)
- Channel-strided byte layout parsing (VmdParser test line 96-107)

### Boundary Conditions
- Frame < first frame (reset to first)
- Frame > last frame (hold last)
- Exact frame match (t=0)
- Between frames (interpolate)
- Frame index hint optimization for forward playback

### Material Shadow Flags
- Tests verify `selfShadow` and `selfShadowMap` flags in material info
- Ground shadow flag handling
- Double-sided material interaction

### Coordinate Conversion
- MMD to Three.js: Y-up preserved, Z-axis flipped for camera position
- Camera roll preservation through Euler angle application
- Target position offset by camera distance in rotated space

---

## Summary Statistics
- **58 total test files** across unit, integration, wasm, and helpers
- **18 files** mentioning camera, light, or shadow
- **9 generated self-shadow PMX models** with 3 associated VMD files
- **12 visual regression test cases** for self-shadow rendering
- **4 public sampling functions** plus 4 "Into" variants (8 total for reusable objects)
- **3 Three.js application functions** (camera, shadow config, shadow fit)

content.text:
## === AGENTS.md ===

```
# AGENTS.md — three-mmd-loader

このファイルは AI コーディングエージェント (Codex / Claude Code / Cursor 等) 向けの
プロジェクト規約集です。**gitignore されています** — リポジトリには含まれません。
リポジトリを使う人間向け規約は README.md / ROADMAP.md を参照。

人間が新しい規約を入れたい時は、このファイルを編集して各 AI ツールの brief で
最初に読ませてください。

---

## ハードルール (違反 = バグ扱い)

### Hot path での allocation 禁止
`runtime.tick()` / `runtime.evaluate()` / `solver.solve*()` / `applyAppendTransforms` /
`applyMmdAnimation` などの **per-frame 経路** で:

- ❌ `new THREE.Vector3()`, `new THREE.Quaternion()`, `new THREE.Matrix4()`
- ❌ `new Float32Array()`, `new Array()`, `[]` の new 生成 (push 累積)
- ❌ `.map()`, `.filter()`, `.slice()` で配列作成
- ✅ `DefaultMmdRuntime` 上の `scratch*` フィールドを `.copy()` / `.set()` で再利用
- ✅ 事前確保した typed array に index 書き込み

### Geometry / texture
- ❌ `geometry.clone()` をフレームごと、または material 数分実行
- ✅ proxy mesh は attribute 参照共有 (`new BufferGeometry()` に attributes/index を参照代入)
- ✅ default texture wrap は `THREE.RepeatWrapping` (MMD UV 規約)
- ✅ Toon gradient は `ClampToEdgeWrapping` 維持
- ✅ default 経路で texture α scan を呼ばない (PMX flag で opaque 確定する場合)

### Transparency 判定
- ✅ PMX flag (diffuse[3] < 1, alphaTest flag, evaluated transparency) を主軸
- ✅ texture α scan は `geometryAwareAlpha: true` のみ
- ❌ texture atlas の余白に α<255 があるだけで transparent: true にしない

### API surface
- ✅ 公開 API (`src/three/index.ts`, `src/runtime/index.ts`, `src/parser/index.ts`,
       `src/physics/index.ts`) からの export だけが利用者向け
- ❌ internal helper を export しない (使われたら破壊的変更が困難になる)
- ✅ 破壊変更は deprecated alias を 1 リリース挟む

---

## 設計指針 (柔軟ルール、状況で判断)

### DRY の濃度
AI コーディング時代は **重複の許容度が変わる**:

| 対象 | 推奨 |
|---|---|
| 1-5 行の trivial helper (`lerp`, `clamp`, `cubicBezier`) | **co-locate OK** (使うファイルに直接書く) |
| 複雑な数学処理 (quaternion 補間、行列変換) | **共有モジュールに集約** (`runtime/math.ts` 等) |
| public API surface | **絶対に重複させない** (利用者が混乱) |
| Test 用 helper | **テストファイル内 co-locate**、必要なら test/helpers/ |

理由: AI は abstraction の解決コストが人間より低いが、共有モジュールへの import +
シグネチャ確認に token を使う。trivial 1 行は inline の方が brief 効率高い。

### ファイルサイズ目安
- `src/**`: モジュール 1 個あたり ~400 行が目安、500 超えたら分割検討
- `examples/viewer/lib/**`: 200-400 行
- `test/**`: 機能ごとに分割 (`SolverLimits.test.ts`, `SolverPrep.test.ts` 等)
- 分割は **type ではなく feature** で (× `types.ts` のみ巨大、○ `animation.ts` に
  関連型と関数を集約)

### コメント
- ❌ コードを読めば自明な what (`// increment counter` 等)
- ✅ why-comments (制約、過去の incident、非自明な不変条件)
- ✅ workaround の context (issue #XX、specific browser bug 等)
- ❌ 「fix for #123」のような瞬間的 context (commit message に書く)

### README 更新
- README の内容調整は **docs/README.ja.md を先に固める**。README.md から
  README.ja.md へ翻訳するのではなく、日本語版を ground truth として英語版
  README.md に反映する。
- ユーザーが README 文言を手動調整した場合、その意図を優先し、英語版へ反映する。
  消された補足や例を勝手に復活させない。

### Release / Viewer deploy
- Release 作業では npm publish / GitHub Release で終わらせず、Viewer のデプロイも行う。
- 詳細なローカル手順と既知の blocker は `docs/DEPLOY.md` を参照する。
- `docs/DEPLOY.md` は `.git/info/exclude` で ignore される自分用 ground truth。
- `docs/RELEASE.md` にはこのローカル運用手順を書かない。

---

## Codex 委譲時の brief テンプレ追加要素

`codex exec` を呼ぶ brief には次を含める:

```text
# 必須参照
- AGENTS.md (最初に読む)
- README.md / ROADMAP.md
- 直近関連 commit (HEAD~5 程度の subject)

# Hot path / API / Geometry を触る場合
- AGENTS.md の該当ハードルールを brief に **引用** する
- 変更後に該当ルール違反していないか SELF-REVIEW で明示
```

---

## Test 戦略

### 単位
- **unit**: 1 関数 / 1 クラス / 純粋ロジック (`test/unit/**`)
- **integration**: 複数モジュール連携 (`test/integration/**`)
- **viewer source-level**: `test/unit/viewer/ViewerSource.test.ts` で文字列マッチ
  により viewer 構造を pin する (回帰防止)

### 何を test するか
- ✅ 公開 API の behavior
- ✅ ハードルール違反の検出 (scratch 未使用、clone 出現 等)
- ❌ internal 構造のスナップショット (リファクタごとに壊れる)
- ❌ 実装詳細 (private method の signature 等)

### Vitest が EPERM で落ちる
Windows + Codex sandbox 環境で Vitest 子プロセス spawn が `EPERM` で失敗することがある。
これは Codex sandbox 制約で本番では起きない。Codex の VERIFICATION では
`npm run build` + 直接実行可能なら手動 dist 確認で代替。**Claude Code 側で
最終 `npm test` を必ず通す**。

---

## Commit / PR

### commit subject 規約
- `feat(scope): ...` 機能追加
- `fix(scope): ...` バグ修正
- `perf(scope): ...` 性能改善
- `refactor(scope): ...` 振る舞い変更なしの整理
- `test(scope): ...` テスト追加・修正
- `docs(scope): ...` 文書のみ
- `chore(scope): ...` build / CI / 設定

scope は `runtime` / `three` / `parser` / `physics` / `viewer` / `readme` 等。

### atomic commit
- 1 commit = 1 関心事
- AI 委譲時に "5 commit に分けて" と指示しても、shared file (index.ts 等) で
  分割困難な場合は **1 big commit** にまとめる方が安全 (subject に複数 scope を併記)

### Co-Authored-By
AI が書いたコードを commit する場合、commit message 末尾に:
```
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
```
または
```
Co-Authored-By: Codex GPT-5.5 <noreply@openai.com>
```
を追記する。

---

## このプロジェクトの最近の判断 (context)

新しいセッションで AI が経緯を知っておくと役立つもの:

- **AnimationClip 経路は完全削除済み** (commit e6d7dfc) — `MmdAnimation` のみ
- **string source は fetch でロード可** (commit c03891a) — `loadModel("/url.pmx")` OK
- **Bezier 補完実装済み** (commit 62fb52b)
- **append transform は PMX layer 順** (commit 2548f66)
- **IK chain validation は setAnimation で 1 回** (commit 820bc08) — solveChain 毎回はしない
- **outline / renderOrder proxy mesh は eager 生成** (commit 6457dca) — `added` event 廃止
- **renderOrderProxies は default OFF** (commit ae64922) — groups sort のみで足りる
- **transparency は PMX flag 優先** (commit a6f604b)
- **texture α scan は default skip** (commit f9ced5e)
- **disposeMmdModel / folder utility / loadAmmoNamespace を library export** (commit 0a6fde2)

### 既知の制約
- Ammo.js 0.0.10 (asm.js) の 64MiB heap 固定
- Codex sandbox で `.git/index.lock` permission denied — commit は Claude Code 側で実行
- Codex の workspace-write BG を **並列実行しない** (workspace 競合)
- Codex output には [BLOCKED: Base64...] が混じる (MCP の安全機能)

---

## このファイルの更新

新規規約・判断・教訓は **このファイルに追加** していくこと。
AI セッションで「同じ ground truth を共有する」ためのファイル。

人間が削除したいルールは削除、追加したい時は append。
gitignore されているため git では追跡されない — チーム共有が必要なら
README.md / ROADMAP.md / CHANGELOG.md に移す。
```

---

## === ROADMAP.md ===

```
# Roadmap

Local-only planning notes for upcoming `@yohawing/three-mmd-loader` work.

Keep this file shaped like `CHANGELOG.md`: group work by target version, then
by intent. Move completed items into `CHANGELOG.md` when they ship.

## Unreleased

### Triage

- Decide whether each item below should stay as a local roadmap item, become a
  GitHub issue, or be deferred until a breaking-release planning pass.
- Keep public README and `docs/README.ja.md` focused on the current supported
  surface; do not restore public roadmap links unless the package needs a
  public planning document again.

## [0.4.0] - Planned

### Changed

- Remove Ammo.js from the default guidance and examples. Keep the backend
  available only as a compatibility path unless a breaking-release decision
  removes it entirely.
- Promote the MMD-optimized Bullet backend as the only recommended physics
  path in README, `docs/README.ja.md`, and viewer-facing examples.
- Resolve the remaining mmd-anim adapter parity gaps so PMX loading no longer
  needs the load-time TypeScript parser fallback in `MmdAnimBackedCore`.
- Bring the TypeScript runtime behavior closer to the mmd-anim/WASM runtime,
  especially for animation sampling, append transforms, IK, morph sync, and
  physics handoff semantics.
- Improve TypeScript runtime performance while preserving the hot-path
  no-allocation rules for `tick`, `evaluate`, IK solving, append transform
  application, and animation application.
- Improve model loading performance across source fetch, PMX/PMD parse,
  geometry/material assembly, texture resolution, and runtime initialization.
- Add an asset dependency resolver that can collect and resolve textures,
  motions, camera motions, audio, accessories, and project-relative paths across
  PMX/PMD/PMM/accessory workflows.
- Add initial accessory support, starting with an explicit scope decision for
  `.x` accessories, `.vac` placement metadata, viewer loading UX, and whether
  accessory attachment belongs in the loader, runtime, or an example/helper
  layer.
- Add partial PMM project support beyond metadata parsing, starting with
  extracting project asset references and enough placement/playback state to
  drive viewer loading for models, motions, camera, audio, and accessories.
- Tune the current MMD-compatible shading path by comparing rendered output
  against MMD dump/reference images, focusing on toon response, diffuse/specular
  balance, ambient terms, alpha handling, outline appearance, and shadow
  interaction.
- Strengthen camera, light, and self-shadow runtime parity by comparing VMD
  sampled state and rendered output against MMD dump/reference evidence.
- Add focused API recipes for common integration paths: model + VMD + camera +
  physics, local files/folders, URL loading, custom runtime, custom physics, and
  viewer/local asset workflows.
- Add a kuroko model preset to the viewer/local asset workflow after confirming
  redistribution terms, attribution text, and whether the model can be bundled
  or must remain a local-only fixture reference.
- Improve diagnostics so loader/runtime/viewer code can distinguish missing
  textures, unsupported texture formats, WASM fallback, unsupported material
  features, skipped physics, accessory resolution failures, and PMM partial
  support gaps with structured data.
- Evolve the viewer into a verification tool with fixed-frame seek,
  diagnostics panels, before/after image capture, MMD dump overlay/reference
  comparison, and repeatable local asset presets.

### Fixed

- Investigate the local fixture oracle mismatches currently skipped in
  `test/fixtures/fixtures.local.json`, especially the Phase5 rig regression
  candidates around PMX knee link-limit/local-axis behavior and dense REM
  IK/twist rigs.
- Separate true runtime IK regressions from native-oracle limitations where
  the local oracle does not apply VMD property IK enable states.

### Test

- Audit the current test suite and classify cases by purpose: public API
  behavior, parser/runtime parity, visual regression, release gate, local-only
  asset verification, and implementation-rule checks.
- Remove or rewrite tests that only pin fragile internal structure without
  protecting meaningful public behavior.
- Add missing coverage for supported behavior that is currently only protected
  by manual viewer checks or local fixture runs.
- Keep local-only fixture tests clearly separated from portable CI/release
  tests so release confidence does not depend on user-owned assets.
- Redefine the release gate after the test audit, explicitly separating
  required portable checks from optional local visual/playback evidence:
  build, lint, unit/integration tests, `smoke:dist`, `smoke:types`, fixture
  checks, visual smoke, and local-only smoke.

### Verification

- Re-run local playback stability coverage after each rig fix and only remove
  `skipReason` entries when the native-oracle expectation is known to be
  meaningful for that case.
- Keep Sour PMX plus `ラビットホール.vmd` as a high-signal regression fixture for
  IK limit and knee-instability checks.
- Compare TypeScript runtime output against the mmd-anim/WASM runtime on
  generated fixtures and selected local playback fixtures before claiming
  parity improvements.
- Track performance changes with runtime benchmarks and allocation-oriented
  source review before accepting hot-path changes.
- Track model loading performance with repeatable benchmarks and viewer timing
  diagnostics before and after parser, texture, or assembly changes.
- Verify asset dependency resolution against portable fixtures and local project
  folders before using it as the foundation for PMM/accessory workflows.
- Validate accessory support with portable minimal fixtures first, then local
  real accessory/model combinations in the viewer before documenting the API.
- Validate partial PMM support with minimal portable PMM fixtures and local
  project files, documenting unsupported fields instead of implying full MMD
  project compatibility.
- Capture before/after image evidence when changing shading behavior and keep
  visual tolerances narrow enough to catch regressions without overfitting to a
  single local model.

## [0.5.0] - Planned

### Design

- Decide whether a rendering pipeline belongs in this package or should live in
  a companion package/example layer. The decision should account for API
  stability, dependency weight, renderer-specific code, and whether consumers
  expect this project to remain a loader/runtime library.
- Sketch a pipeline boundary for MMD image making before adding shading helpers:
  scene lighting, material conversion/enhancement, outline/self-shadow,
  post-processing, renderer selection, and viewer presets should have explicit
  ownership.
- Consider an opt-in `MmdRenderingPipeline` style API only if it can compose
  with existing `ThreeMmdLoader` output without taking over the user's whole
  Three.js scene.

### Added

- Explore a Three.js WebGPU rendering path for MMD models, keeping the existing
  WebGL path as the default until material, outline, morph, skinning, and
  self-shadow behavior have parity evidence.
- Prototype WGSL-backed shader pieces where they provide a clear benefit over
  Three.js' built-in WebGPU node/material path, especially for MMD-specific
  material evaluation, toon handling, or future skinning/runtime experiments.
- Evaluate style-helper APIs for richer MMD shading presets inspired by rayMMD
  and sd PBR workflows, without claiming drop-in compatibility with those
  effect stacks.
- Prototype opt-in material/lighting helpers for PBR-like image making:
  environment lighting setup, shadow tuning, toon-to-PBR material parameter
  mapping, rim/specular controls, and post-processing-friendly defaults.

### Verification

- Validate the pipeline ownership decision with at least one app-style viewer
  prototype before committing to public API.
- Add visual parity checks that compare WebGL and WebGPU output for generated
  PMX material cases before exposing WebGPU as a documented viewer option.
- Extend the viewer verification workflow to switch WebGL/WebGPU paths and
  capture comparable frames for renderer and shading experiments.
- Treat WebGPU support as experimental until browser support, Three.js API
  stability, and package ergonomics are confirmed against typed consumer smoke
  coverage.
- Validate shading helpers with real MMD models in the local viewer and keep
  them opt-in so the default MMD-compatible material path remains stable.

## [1.0.0] - Planned

### Removed

- Remove deprecated aliases after one release window:
  `ThreeMmdModel.object`, `ThreeMmdModel.textureDiagnostics`,
  `ThreeMmdLoadModelOptions.outlines`, and
  `ThreeMmdLoadModelOptions.renderOrderProxies`.
- Remove or replace deprecated runtime APIs only after confirming downstream
  consumers have a clear migration path:
  `tick(seconds, mesh, options)` and broad `stop()` usage.
- Decide whether `AmmoPhysicsBackendTuningOptions.additionalDampingPatch`
  should be removed with the rest of the deprecated compatibility aliases.

### Changed

- Revisit the public physics API once the default path is firmly the direct
  MMD Bullet backend and the legacy Ammo.js path is no longer part of standard
  documentation.

### Verification

- Before cutting the breaking release, run the release gate with typed consumer
  smoke coverage across root and subpath imports.
```

## === docs/RELEASE.md ===

```
# Release

This checklist is the operator-facing release procedure for
`@yohawing/three-mmd-loader`. Development details for individual scripts live in
[DEVELOPMENT.md](./DEVELOPMENT.md).

## 1. Preflight

- Confirm the target version and release scope.
- Confirm `CHANGELOG.md` has an entry for the target version.
- Build the `CHANGELOG.md` entry from the commits since the previous release
  tag, not only from the final release-prep commit.
- Confirm `package.json` `version` matches the intended release.
- Confirm the working tree is clean.
- Confirm the release branch is based on `develop`.
- Confirm npm Trusted Publishing is configured for the GitHub `npm`
  environment.

```bash
git status --short
npm ci
```

## 2. Local Checks

Run the same release-blocking checks that CI runs before packaging:

```bash
npm run lint
npm test
npm run build
npm run check:fixtures
npm run smoke:dist
npm run smoke:types
npm pack --dry-run --json
```

If the `native/third_party/mmd-anim` submodule or the WASM export surface changed,
rebuild the WASM wrapper before the normal build:

```bash
npm run build:mmd-anim
npm run build
```

If this release changes rendering (material/outline/shader) or the mmd-anim
parser/runtime/WASM artifacts, also refresh and review the generated-PMX visual
baselines before tagging. They are **not** CI-gated (GPU/platform-specific
output, see [DEVELOPMENT.md](./DEVELOPMENT.md) → Visual Regression Scripts) and
can otherwise ship stale:

```bash
npm run visual:smoke:generated-pmx
# if rendering legitimately changed: regenerate, review the PNG diffs, re-run
# the smoke until green, then commit the reviewed baselines
npm run render:visual:generated-pmx:baseline
```

## 3. Commit

Commit the version, changelog, and any release-prep changes before tagging.

```bash
git status --short
git add package.json package-lock.json CHANGELOG.md
git commit -m "chore(release): vX.Y.Z"
```

Adjust the staged files if the release includes additional documentation or
source changes.

Push the release-prep commit to `develop` before opening the release PR.

```bash
git push origin develop
```

## 4. Merge `develop` to `main`

Open a pull request from `develop` to `main` before tagging. Do not publish from
`develop`; the release tag must be created from the reviewed and merged `main`
commit.

```bash
gh pr create --base main --head develop --title "chore(release): vX.Y.Z"
```

Review the PR, wait for CI, and merge it into `main`. Direct pushes to `main`
may be blocked by repository rules; use the PR path even when the merge is a
fast-forward.

After merging, update the local `main` branch and confirm that `package.json`
still matches the intended version.

```bash
git fetch origin
git switch main
git pull --ff-only origin main
node -e "const p=require('./package.json'); if (p.version !== 'X.Y.Z') process.exit(1)"
```

## 5. Tag

The release workflow requires the Git tag to match `package.json` exactly.
Create the tag on `main` after the release PR has been reviewed and merged.

```bash
git tag vX.Y.Z
git push origin vX.Y.Z
```

For example, `package.json` version `0.2.0` must be tagged as `v0.2.0`.

## 6. GitHub Actions

The `Release` workflow runs on `v*.*.*` tags. It builds the package, validates
tag/version consistency, creates the npm tarball, publishes to npm, and creates
or updates the GitHub Release.

Verify these workflow results:

- The package job passed `lint`, `test`, `build`, fixture checks, dist smoke,
  and TypeScript consumer smoke.
- The publish job ran under the GitHub `npm` environment.
- npm Trusted Publishing succeeded.
- The GitHub Release exists for the tag, has the package artifact attached, and
  uses the matching `CHANGELOG.md` version section as its release notes.

The release workflow extracts the `## [X.Y.Z]` section from `CHANGELOG.md` for
tag `vX.Y.Z`. If that section is missing, the workflow fails instead of
publishing generic generated notes.

Manual dispatch is also available, but use tag-triggered releases for normal
publishing so the GitHub Release is tied to the version tag.

## 7. Post-release Verification

Verify the published npm artifact rather than only the local package:

```bash
npm view @yohawing/three-mmd-loader version
npm view @yohawing/three-mmd-loader peerDependencies
npm view @yohawing/three-mmd-loader dist-tags
```

When compatibility matters for a downstream app, install the published version
there or in a temporary consumer before updating that app.
```

---

## === AGENTS.md ===

```
# AGENTS.md — three-mmd-loader

このファイルは AI コーディングエージェント (Codex / Claude Code / Cursor 等) 向けの
プロジェクト規約集です。**gitignore されています** — リポジトリには含まれません。
リポジトリを使う人間向け規約は README.md / ROADMAP.md を参照。

人間が新しい規約を入れたい時は、このファイルを編集して各 AI ツールの brief で
最初に読ませてください。

---

## ハードルール (違反 = バグ扱い)

### Hot path での allocation 禁止
`runtime.tick()` / `runtime.evaluate()` / `solver.solve*()` / `applyAppendTransforms` /
`applyMmdAnimation` などの **per-frame 経路** で:

- ❌ `new THREE.Vector3()`, `new THREE.Quaternion()`, `new THREE.Matrix4()`
- ❌ `new Float32Array()`, `new Array()`, `[]` の new 生成 (push 累積)
- ❌ `.map()`, `.filter()`, `.slice()` で配列作成
- ✅ `DefaultMmdRuntime` 上の `scratch*` フィールドを `.copy()` / `.set()` で再利用
- ✅ 事前確保した typed array に index 書き込み

### Geometry / texture
- ❌ `geometry.clone()` をフレームごと、または material 数分実行
- ✅ proxy mesh は attribute 参照共有 (`new BufferGeometry()` に attributes/index を参照代入)
- ✅ default texture wrap は `THREE.RepeatWrapping` (MMD UV 規約)
- ✅ Toon gradient は `ClampToEdgeWrapping` 維持
- ✅ default 経路で texture α scan を呼ばない (PMX flag で opaque 確定する場合)

### Transparency 判定
- ✅ PMX flag (diffuse[3] < 1, alphaTest flag, evaluated transparency) を主軸
- ✅ texture α scan は `geometryAwareAlpha: true` のみ
- ❌ texture atlas の余白に α<255 があるだけで transparent: true にしない

### API surface
- ✅ 公開 API (`src/three/index.ts`, `src/runtime/index.ts`, `src/parser/index.ts`,
       `src/physics/index.ts`) からの export だけが利用者向け
- ❌ internal helper を export しない (使われたら破壊的変更が困難になる)
- ✅ 破壊変更は deprecated alias を 1 リリース挟む

---

## 設計指針 (柔軟ルール、状況で判断)

### DRY の濃度
AI コーディング時代は **重複の許容度が変わる**:

| 対象 | 推奨 |
|---|---|
| 1-5 行の trivial helper (`lerp`, `clamp`, `cubicBezier`) | **co-locate OK** (使うファイルに直接書く) |
| 複雑な数学処理 (quaternion 補間、行列変換) | **共有モジュールに集約** (`runtime/math.ts` 等) |
| public API surface | **絶対に重複させない** (利用者が混乱) |
| Test 用 helper | **テストファイル内 co-locate**、必要なら test/helpers/ |

理由: AI は abstraction の解決コストが人間より低いが、共有モジュールへの import +
シグネチャ確認に token を使う。trivial 1 行は inline の方が brief 効率高い。

### ファイルサイズ目安
- `src/**`: モジュール 1 個あたり ~400 行が目安、500 超えたら分割検討
- `examples/viewer/lib/**`: 200-400 行
- `test/**`: 機能ごとに分割 (`SolverLimits.test.ts`, `SolverPrep.test.ts` 等)
- 分割は **type ではなく feature** で (× `types.ts` のみ巨大、○ `animation.ts` に
  関連型と関数を集約)

### コメント
- ❌ コードを読めば自明な what (`// increment counter` 等)
- ✅ why-comments (制約、過去の incident、非自明な不変条件)
- ✅ workaround の context (issue #XX、specific browser bug 等)
- ❌ 「fix for #123」のような瞬間的 context (commit message に書く)

### README 更新
- README の内容調整は **docs/README.ja.md を先に固める**。README.md から
  README.ja.md へ翻訳するのではなく、日本語版を ground truth として英語版
  README.md に反映する。
- ユーザーが README 文言を手動調整した場合、その意図を優先し、英語版へ反映する。
  消された補足や例を勝手に復活させない。

### Release / Viewer deploy
- Release 作業では npm publish / GitHub Release で終わらせず、Viewer のデプロイも行う。
- 詳細なローカル手順と既知の blocker は `docs/DEPLOY.md` を参照する。
- `docs/DEPLOY.md` は `.git/info/exclude` で ignore される自分用 ground truth。
- `docs/RELEASE.md` にはこのローカル運用手順を書かない。

---

## Codex 委譲時の brief テンプレ追加要素

`codex exec` を呼ぶ brief には次を含める:

```text
# 必須参照
- AGENTS.md (最初に読む)
- README.md / ROADMAP.md
- 直近関連 commit (HEAD~5 程度の subject)

# Hot path / API / Geometry を触る場合
- AGENTS.md の該当ハードルールを brief に **引用** する
- 変更後に該当ルール違反していないか SELF-REVIEW で明示
```

---

## Test 戦略

### 単位
- **unit**: 1 関数 / 1 クラス / 純粋ロジック (`test/unit/**`)
- **integration**: 複数モジュール連携 (`test/integration/**`)
- **viewer source-level**: `test/unit/viewer/ViewerSource.test.ts` で文字列マッチ
  により viewer 構造を pin する (回帰防止)

### 何を test するか
- ✅ 公開 API の behavior
- ✅ ハードルール違反の検出 (scratch 未使用、clone 出現 等)
- ❌ internal 構造のスナップショット (リファクタごとに壊れる)
- ❌ 実装詳細 (private method の signature 等)

### Vitest が EPERM で落ちる
Windows + Codex sandbox 環境で Vitest 子プロセス spawn が `EPERM` で失敗することがある。
これは Codex sandbox 制約で本番では起きない。Codex の VERIFICATION では
`npm run build` + 直接実行可能なら手動 dist 確認で代替。**Claude Code 側で
最終 `npm test` を必ず通す**。

---

## Commit / PR

### commit subject 規約
- `feat(scope): ...` 機能追加
- `fix(scope): ...` バグ修正
- `perf(scope): ...` 性能改善
- `refactor(scope): ...` 振る舞い変更なしの整理
- `test(scope): ...` テスト追加・修正
- `docs(scope): ...` 文書のみ
- `chore(scope): ...` build / CI / 設定

scope は `runtime` / `three` / `parser` / `physics` / `viewer` / `readme` 等。

### atomic commit
- 1 commit = 1 関心事
- AI 委譲時に "5 commit に分けて" と指示しても、shared file (index.ts 等) で
  分割困難な場合は **1 big commit** にまとめる方が安全 (subject に複数 scope を併記)

### Co-Authored-By
AI が書いたコードを commit する場合、commit message 末尾に:
```
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
```
または
```
Co-Authored-By: Codex GPT-5.5 <noreply@openai.com>
```
を追記する。

## このプロジェクトの最近の判断 (context)

新しいセッションで AI が経緯を知っておくと役立つもの:

- **AnimationClip 経路は完全削除済み** (commit e6d7dfc) — `MmdAnimation` のみ
- **string source は fetch でロード可** (commit c03891a) — `loadModel("/url.pmx")` OK
- **Bezier 補完実装済み** (commit 62fb52b)
- **append transform は PMX layer 順** (commit 2548f66)
- **IK chain validation は setAnimation で 1 回** (commit 820bc08) — solveChain 毎回はしない
- **outline / renderOrder proxy mesh は eager 生成** (commit 6457dca) — `added` event 廃止
- **renderOrderProxies は default OFF** (commit ae64922) — groups sort のみで足りる
- **transparency は PMX flag 優先** (commit a6f604b)
- **texture α scan は default skip** (commit f9ced5e)
- **disposeMmdModel / folder utility / loadAmmoNamespace を library export** (commit 0a6fde2)

### 既知の制約
- Ammo.js 0.0.10 (asm.js) の 64MiB heap 固定
- Codex sandbox で `.git/index.lock` permission denied — commit は Claude Code 側で実行
- Codex の workspace-write BG を **並列実行しない** (workspace 競合)
- Codex output には [BLOCKED: Base64...] が混じる (MCP の安全機能)

## === ROADMAP.md ===

```
# Roadmap

Local-only planning notes for upcoming `@yohawing/three-mmd-loader` work.

Keep this file shaped like `CHANGELOG.md`: group work by target version, then
by intent. Move completed items into `CHANGELOG.md` when they ship.

## Unreleased

### Triage

- Decide whether each item below should stay as a local roadmap item, become a
  GitHub issue, or be deferred until a breaking-release planning pass.
- Keep public README and `docs/README.ja.md` focused on the current supported
  surface; do not restore public roadmap links unless the package needs a
  public planning document again.

## [0.4.0] - Planned

### Changed

- Remove Ammo.js from the default guidance and examples. Keep the backend
  available only as a compatibility path unless a breaking-release decision
  removes it entirely.
- Promote the MMD-optimized Bullet backend as the only recommended physics
  path in README, `docs/README.ja.md`, and viewer-facing examples.
- Resolve the remaining mmd-anim adapter parity gaps so PMX loading no longer
  needs the load-time TypeScript parser fallback in `MmdAnimBackedCore`.
- Bring the TypeScript runtime behavior closer to the mmd-anim/WASM runtime,
  especially for animation sampling, append transforms, IK, morph sync, and
  physics handoff semantics.
- Improve TypeScript runtime performance while preserving the hot-path
  no-allocation rules for `tick`, `evaluate`, IK solving, append transform
  application, and animation application.
- Improve model loading performance across source fetch, PMX/PMD parse,
  geometry/material assembly, texture resolution, and runtime initialization.
- Add an asset dependency resolver that can collect and resolve textures,
  motions, camera motions, audio, accessories, and project-relative paths across
  PMX/PMD/PMM/accessory workflows.
- Add initial accessory support, starting with an explicit scope decision for
  `.x` accessories, `.vac` placement metadata, viewer loading UX, and whether
  accessory attachment belongs in the loader, runtime, or an example/helper
  layer.
- Add partial PMM project support beyond metadata parsing, starting with
  extracting project asset references and enough placement/playback state to
  drive viewer loading for models, motions, camera, audio, and accessories.
- Tune the current MMD-compatible shading path by comparing rendered output
  against MMD dump/reference images, focusing on toon response, diffuse/specular
  balance, ambient terms, alpha handling, outline appearance, and shadow
  interaction.
- Strengthen camera, light, and self-shadow runtime parity by comparing VMD
  sampled state and rendered output against MMD dump/reference evidence.
- Add focused API recipes for common integration paths: model + VMD + camera +
  physics, local files/folders, URL loading, custom runtime, custom physics, and
  viewer/local asset workflows.
- Add a kuroko model preset to the viewer/local asset workflow after confirming
  redistribution terms, attribution text, and whether the model can be bundled
  or must remain a local-only fixture reference.
- Improve diagnostics so loader/runtime/viewer code can distinguish missing
  textures, unsupported texture formats, WASM fallback, unsupported material
  features, skipped physics, accessory resolution failures, and PMM partial
  support gaps with structured data.
- Evolve the viewer into a verification tool with fixed-frame seek,
  diagnostics panels, before/after image capture, MMD dump overlay/reference
  comparison, and repeatable local asset presets.

### Fixed

- Investigate the local fixture oracle mismatches currently skipped in
  `test/fixtures/fixtures.local.json`, especially the Phase5 rig regression
  candidates around PMX knee link-limit/local-axis behavior and dense REM
  IK/twist rigs.
- Separate true runtime IK regressions from native-oracle limitations where
  the local oracle does not apply VMD property IK enable states.

### Test

- Audit the current test suite and classify cases by purpose: public API
  behavior, parser/runtime parity, visual regression, release gate, local-only
  asset verification, and implementation-rule checks.
- Remove or rewrite tests that only pin fragile internal structure without
  protecting meaningful public behavior.
- Add missing coverage for supported behavior that is currently only protected
  by manual viewer checks or local fixture runs.
- Keep local-only fixture tests clearly separated from portable CI/release
  tests so release confidence does not depend on user-owned assets.
- Redefine the release gate after the test audit, explicitly separating
  required portable checks from optional local visual/playback evidence:
  build, lint, unit/integration tests, `smoke:dist`, `smoke:types`, fixture
  checks, visual smoke, and local-only smoke.

### Verification

- Re-run local playback stability coverage after each rig fix and only remove
  `skipReason` entries when the native-oracle expectation is known to be
  meaningful for that case.
- Keep Sour PMX plus `ラビットホール.vmd` as a high-signal regression fixture for
  IK limit and knee-instability checks.
- Compare TypeScript runtime output against the mmd-anim/WASM runtime on
  generated fixtures and selected local playback fixtures before claiming
  parity improvements.
- Track performance changes with runtime benchmarks and allocation-oriented
  source review before accepting hot-path changes.
- Track model loading performance with repeatable benchmarks and viewer timing
  diagnostics before and after parser, texture, or assembly changes.
- Verify asset dependency resolution against portable fixtures and local project
  folders before using it as the foundation for PMM/accessory workflows.
- Validate accessory support with portable minimal fixtures first, then local
  real accessory/model combinations in the viewer before documenting the API.
- Validate partial PMM support with minimal portable PMM fixtures and local
  project files, documenting unsupported fields instead of implying full MMD
  project compatibility.
- Capture before/after image evidence when changing shading behavior and keep
  visual tolerances narrow enough to catch regressions without overfitting to a
  single local model.

## [0.5.0] - Planned

### Design

- Decide whether a rendering pipeline belongs in this package or should live in
  a companion package/example layer. The decision should account for API
  stability, dependency weight, renderer-specific code, and whether consumers
  expect this project to remain a loader/runtime library.
- Sketch a pipeline boundary for MMD image making before adding shading helpers:
  scene lighting, material conversion/enhancement, outline/self-shadow,
  post-processing, renderer selection, and viewer presets should have explicit
  ownership.
- Consider an opt-in `MmdRenderingPipeline` style API only if it can compose
  with existing `ThreeMmdLoader` output without taking over the user's whole
  Three.js scene.

### Added

- Explore a Three.js WebGPU rendering path for MMD models, keeping the existing
  WebGL path as the default until material, outline, morph, skinning, and
  self-shadow behavior have parity evidence.
- Prototype WGSL-backed shader pieces where they provide a clear benefit over
  Three.js' built-in WebGPU node/material path, especially for MMD-specific
  material evaluation, toon handling, or future skinning/runtime experiments.
- Evaluate style-helper APIs for richer MMD shading presets inspired by rayMMD
  and sd PBR workflows, without claiming drop-in compatibility with those
  effect stacks.
- Prototype opt-in material/lighting helpers for PBR-like image making:
  environment lighting setup, shadow tuning, toon-to-PBR material parameter
  mapping, rim/specular controls, and post-processing-friendly defaults.

### Verification

- Validate the pipeline ownership decision with at least one app-style viewer
  prototype before committing to public API.
- Add visual parity checks that compare WebGL and WebGPU output for generated
  PMX material cases before exposing WebGPU as a documented viewer option.
- Extend the viewer verification workflow to switch WebGL/WebGPU paths and
  capture comparable frames for renderer and shading experiments.
- Treat WebGPU support as experimental until browser support, Three.js API
  stability, and package ergonomics are confirmed against typed consumer smoke
  coverage.
- Validate shading helpers with real MMD models in the local viewer and keep
  them opt-in so the default MMD-compatible material path remains stable.

## [1.0.0] - Planned

### Removed

- Remove deprecated aliases after one release window:
  `ThreeMmdModel.object`, `ThreeMmdModel.textureDiagnostics`,
  `ThreeMmdLoadModelOptions.outlines`, and
  `ThreeMmdLoadModelOptions.renderOrderProxies`.
- Remove or replace deprecated runtime APIs only after confirming downstream
  consumers have a clear migration path:
  `tick(seconds, mesh, options)` and broad `stop()` usage.
- Decide whether `AmmoPhysicsBackendTuningOptions.additionalDampingPatch`
  should be removed with the rest of the deprecated compatibility aliases.

### Changed

- Revisit the public physics API once the default path is firmly the direct
  MMD Bullet backend and the legacy Ammo.js path is no longer part of standard
  documentation.

### Verification

- Before cutting the breaking release, run the release gate with typed consumer
  smoke coverage across root and subpath imports.
```
